name: Chesterfield County Public Schools
districtFact: Largest school district in the Richmond metropolitan area, serving the suburban communities south and west of Virginia's capital city.

about.title: About the District
about.content: Chesterfield County Public Schools serves approximately 64,000 students across 65 schools in Chesterfield County, Virginia — the largest school district in the Richmond metropolitan area.

planningTips: Staggered first day: elementary students and 9th graders start Aug 24; 7th–8th graders and 10th–12th graders start Aug 25. Plan childcare and transportation separately for different grades in your household.
Good Friday (Mar 26) is a school holiday in CCPS, immediately before Spring Break. Combined with Spring Break (Mar 29–Apr 2), families have a 10-day window — plan travel accordingly.
CCPS uses a four-quarter system with early-release days at quarter ends. Get the full list of three-hour early dismissal days from the school office at the start of the year.
Chesterfield County is separate from Richmond City and Henrico County school divisions. Verify your school division using your specific street address at ccpsnet.net.

districtFaqs.q: What is the first day of school for Chesterfield County Public Schools in 2026?
districtFaqs.a: Chesterfield County Public Schools uses a staggered first-day schedule. Elementary students (grades 1–5), 6th graders, and 9th graders begin August 24, 2026. Students in grades 7–8 and 10–12 begin August 25, 2026. The staggered schedule is designed to help transitioning students (entering middle and high school) get oriented before the full school population arrives.

districtFaqs.q: Is Chesterfield County Public Schools the same as Richmond City Public Schools?
districtFaqs.a: No. Chesterfield County Public Schools and Richmond City Public Schools are separate, independent school divisions serving different jurisdictions in the Richmond metropolitan area. They have different school boards, calendars, and school boundaries. Families new to the Richmond area should confirm which school division serves their specific address at each division's website.

districtFaqs.q: What is the four-quarter system in Chesterfield County?
districtFaqs.a: Chesterfield County Public Schools organizes the academic year into four quarters rather than two traditional semesters. Quarter-end dates in 2026-2027 fall around October 30 (Q1), January 22 (Q2), March 25 (Q3), and June 4 (Q4/last day). Each quarter end is associated with early-release days and parent conference periods. The quarter-based grading system affects how report cards and grade reporting work throughout the year.

districtFaqs.q: Why does CCPS stagger the first day of school?
districtFaqs.a: Chesterfield County Public Schools staggers the first day to give staff extra time to support students who are transitioning to a new school level. Elementary students (grades 1–5), 6th graders entering middle school, and 9th graders entering high school begin August 24, 2026. Students in grades 7–8 and 10–12 begin August 25. This means that on the first day, staff can focus on orientating new-to-level students before the full school population arrives. Households with children across multiple grade levels need separate childcare and transportation arrangements on August 24.

districtFaqs.q: Does CCPS follow the same calendar as Henrico County Public Schools?
districtFaqs.a: No. Chesterfield County Public Schools and Henrico County Public Schools are separate, independent school divisions with different academic calendars. While both typically start and end within a few days of each other, specific dates for teacher workdays, early-release days, and spring break may differ. Families with children in both divisions — or coordinating with grandparents or childcare providers in Henrico — should verify each division's calendar separately each year.

districtFaqs.q: Is Good Friday always a school holiday in Chesterfield County?
districtFaqs.a: Good Friday is observed as a school holiday in Chesterfield County Public Schools and is a longstanding practice in Virginia's public school divisions. In 2026-2027, Good Friday falls on March 26 — the Friday immediately before Spring Break (March 29–April 2). This creates a 10-day break window. Families relocating from states where Good Friday is not a school holiday may not expect this day off; it is listed on the official CCPS calendar.

districtFaqs.q: What are three-hour early release days in CCPS?
districtFaqs.a: Three-hour early release days (also called three-hour early dismissal days) are scheduled throughout the school year in Chesterfield County for teacher professional development and parent conferences. On these days, students are released approximately three hours earlier than the normal dismissal time. These days are distinct from the regularly scheduled half days and typically cluster around the end of each academic quarter (around October 30, January 22, and March 25). Get the full list of early release dates from your school's office at the start of the year, as the exact schedule can vary by school.

livingHere.intro: Chesterfield County wraps around the south and west of Richmond, Virginia's state capital, offering suburban living with easy access to Richmond's cultural institutions, the James River, and I-95 — at significantly lower cost than Northern Virginia. The county is undergoing an economic transition away from its tobacco-industry past toward healthcare, logistics, and financial services.

highlights.label: Richmond International Airport (RIC)
highlights.detail: RIC is 20–30 minutes from most Chesterfield neighborhoods and is the departure point for most CCPS family travel. The school calendar defines the three busiest booking windows: Thanksgiving week (CCPS observes the full week), the winter break period around Christmas, and the stretch from CCPS's June 4 last day through early July. Book holiday flights as soon as the CCPS calendar is released each spring — RIC has fewer nonstop options than Dulles or BWI, so fares and award seats on peak-season dates fill faster.

highlights.label: James River — Summer and End-of-Year
highlights.detail: The James River is the dominant end-of-school and summer destination for Chesterfield families. Youth paddling programs and river camps near Pony Pasture Rapids and Huguenot Flatwater fill in March and April — register as soon as spring break begins on March 29 to secure summer spots. Kayak and canoe rentals along the James book out quickly the weekend after CCPS's June 4 last day. The river also features in the CCPS science and outdoor education curriculum, particularly in middle school environmental studies units.

highlights.label: Civil War Heritage and CCPS Field Trips
highlights.detail: Richmond served as the Confederate capital, and Chesterfield borders major battlefields including Cold Harbor and the Petersburg National Battlefield. These sites are central to CCPS's Virginia Studies curriculum: 4th and 5th graders regularly take curriculum-tied field trips to the battlefields and Richmond's Civil War landmarks as part of Virginia history requirements. Families new to the district should expect at least one battlefield or Richmond history field trip per year in elementary grades — permission slips and chaperone signups typically go out 2–3 weeks in advance.

highlights.label: Healthcare Sector and Mid-Year Enrollment
highlights.detail: HCA Virginia, VCU Health, and Bon Secours Richmond operate major facilities in or adjacent to Chesterfield, drawing a steady stream of medical professionals and hospital staff to the county. Many healthcare employees arrive on rotating schedules or mid-year transfer assignments — CCPS regularly processes enrollment outside the traditional August window. Families relocating for hospital positions should contact CCPS enrollment before their start date; the district can typically place students within days of a completed application.

highlights.label: Staggered First Day of School
highlights.detail: CCPS uses a phased school opening — elementary, 6th, and 9th graders start August 24; other grades start August 25 — so households with children at multiple grade levels need separate first-day childcare and transportation plans.

highlights.label: Kings Dominion — Season and School Calendar
highlights.detail: Kings Dominion, Virginia's largest amusement park, is 25 miles north of Midlothian in Doswell and a regular CCPS family destination. Season passes bought in winter pay off quickly: Opening Weekend (typically late March or early April) falls just before CCPS's spring break window (March 29–April 2), giving pass holders their lowest-crowd visits of the season. The park runs through October, overlapping with the first quarter of school — weekend visits are common through Q1 before homework and activity schedules increase. CCPS school groups occasionally book end-of-year trips in late May.

highlights.label: Midlothian — Where Families Relocate First
highlights.detail: Midlothian is where most families new to Chesterfield County choose to live — it's the densest suburban corridor, with established neighborhoods served by multiple well-regarded elementary and high schools. Fall registration in Midlothian fills quickly: popular elementary schools in the Hull Street Road and Coalfield Road corridors reach capacity in early August. Families relocating from out of state should contact CCPS enrollment by July to confirm school assignment and supply lists ahead of the August 24 staggered first day. New residents should also allow extra time to learn school-day traffic patterns on Midlothian Turnpike, which backs up significantly during morning drop-off.

highlights.label: Quarter-End Rhythms for CCPS Families
highlights.detail: Chesterfield's four-quarter calendar creates a predictable annual rhythm that shapes family scheduling. Parent-teacher conferences and three-hour early release days cluster around each quarter end — October 30, January 22, and March 25 — so arranging afternoon childcare on those dates is a recurring need. Report cards are issued at each quarter's close, giving families four check-ins per year rather than two. Families new to a quarter-based system (especially those coming from semester-based districts in other states) should download the CCPS academic calendar on the first day of school and mark all early-release and conference dates immediately.
